arts.ttl: VAR audio of decision not to show Azpilcueta red card for Raphinha challenge released
arts.sum: Referee José Luis Munuera Montero was asked by VAR to check the incident on the screen but decided against upgrading his yellow card decision to a red. Barcelona booked their place in the Copa del Rey final on Wednesday with a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid that completed a 5-4 aggregate victory .

arts.ttl: Brave Barca set up classic Copa final after awesome Atleti showing
arts.sum: The slender margin of the win makes a path to an El Clasico final in the Copa del Rey all the sweeter, and it continued the epic run which has seen Hansi Flick’s side remain unbeaten in 2025. Consistent purposeful running at the heart of Los Colchoneros defence from Frenkie de Jong meant that Atleti’s midfield were on the back foot for long periods, whilst Pedri’s unbelievable trickery with the ball ensured that Barca kept possession for the most part.

arts.ttl: ‘Dreaming is allowed’ - Flick speaks out after Barca beat Atletico
arts.sum: Hansi Flick says “dreaming is allowed” after Barcelona booked their spot in the Copa del Rey final by beating Atletico Madrid . I think it’s one of the best teams, not just in Spain but in Europe, but yes we are happy.

arts.ttl: Barcelona vs Real Madrid: Copa del Rey final prediction, date, kick-off time, TV, live stream, h2h results
arts.sum: Huge Copa del Rey final is on the horizon

arts.ttl: Diego Simeone explains thoughts on ‘failure’ before facing Barcelona in season-defining semi-final
arts.sum: “The team has competed very well so far, in the Champions League we competed very well, in the cup as well, we are having a good league season and we will continue game by game. I repeat that we have been good in the Champions League, in the cup, we lost 4-5 against a rival that plays very well and deserved to go through, and in the league we are competing well and we will continue in the same way.

arts.ttl: “My knee was in a completely different position” – Barcelona star opens up on horror injury
arts.sum: It has been a tremendous season for Barcelona up until now, and it is made more impressive considering they have been without their captain for almost the entire campaign. Back in September, Marc-Andre Ter Stegen suffered a serious knee injury after a freak incident during Barcelona’s 5-1 victory over Villarreal at La Ceramica. The German […] The post “My knee was in a completely different position” – Barcelona star opens up on horror injury appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Torres sends Barcelona past Atlético and into clásico Copa del Rey final
arts.sum: Semi-final: Atlético Madrid 0-1 Barcelona (agg 4-5) Barça v Real Madrid final for first time since 2014 More than a decade later, the Copa del Rey will have a clásico final. First Barcelona played, then they resisted, expertly suppressing Atlético Madrid’s brief rebellion at the Metropolitano, and together those two halves, those two faces, took them through. A wild and open first leg, 4-4 at Montjuic, gave way to a tighter second won by a single Ferran Torres goal. Seville awaits football’s greatest rivals, both of them still chasing a treble. “Dreaming is allowed,” Hansi Flick said, “but we will have to work hard. At the club they have a lot of space for more titles.” For Diego Simeone’s side, meanwhile, this was The End. In the five weeks since the first leg of this semi-final, a season that had set up to be superb instead escaped Atlético, all three major competitions gone. They have won just one of five games since then, and that was the Champions League second leg in which Madrid knocked them out on penalties. They also slipped nine points off the top in La Liga and now their cup run is over. They had taken their opponents to the line but could not get over it themselves. “There’s nothing to reproach,” the coach said. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Yamal left with bloodied ankle after Lenglet challenge
arts.sum: Lamine Yamal was left bloodied by Clement Lenglet during Barcelona’s Copa del Rey tie with Atletico Madrid on Wednesday night . Yamal was then crunched by Lenglet in the second half and was left bleeding by the Barcelona loanee.

arts.ttl: What happened the last time Barcelona faced Real Madrid in the Copa del Rey final?
arts.sum: The next edition of the biggest rivalry in Spanish football was officially pencilled in on Wednesday night. This comes after Barcelona booked their spot in the finale of the Copa…

arts.ttl: Barcelona book El Clásico Copa del Rey final against Real Madrid
arts.sum: Barcelona will face Real Madrid in the Copa del Rey final after a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid. Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the game in the Spanish capital to keep the Catalan’s dream of a treble alive this season.
